'''Bongo Missiles''' were aquatic anti-[[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] vessels of the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. They were based on [[Tribubble bongo/Legends|tribubble bongos]], but were armed with a surface-to-air [[Missile launcher/Legends|missile launcher]] to defend against enemy aircraft. They were, however, defenseless against other ships or ground forces. The ship's main body was crescent-shaped and had two separate [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpits]], and were driven by two propellers on outriggers. Bongo Missiles were constructed at [[Shipyard (water)|shipyards]] for a cost of 100 units of [[Carbon/Legends|carbon]] and 35 units of [[Nova crystal/Legends|nova crystals]], and could be upgraded into a more powerful vessel, the [[Bongo Missile|Bongo Heavy Missile]]. Bongo Missiles saw action against the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|occupation]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].

==Behind the scenes==
Bongo Missiles appear in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' and its [[2002]] [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns|expansion pack]]. They are the Gungan anti-air naval units.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' {{1st}}
*''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]''
